sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 7/10 in severity, has been experiencing neck pain since a car accident one week ago, where he was hit from behind and his neck and head were forced forward rapidly.
The pain is in the back of the neck and worsens with movement, but has not changed in severity over time.
He has tried ibuprofen with some relief, but it only brought his pain down to 5/10.
He has also had occasional headaches since the accident.
He denies any hearing or visual disturbances and has no history of neck problems from sports or previous accidents.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient denies any additional symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient has positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, positive pain with flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending of the neck.
Test Results: The patient's x-ray shows no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a neck strain, also known as whiplash, due to the recent car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; suspected whiplash.
Plan:
I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes.
I also prescribed Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
I advised the patient to be conservative with neck movement and avoid straining the neck.
I ordered an MRI to rule out any underlying conditions such as concussion or nerve impingement.
I referred the patient to either physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment after the MRI results are in.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me once the MRI results are available.
